note: AUD5 fix for finding F-15 (docs/novakai/audit/04-findings.md). SCOPE: .mjs TOOLING test behaviour — real verification is node --test tools/buildspec/conformance-strict.test.mjs (the strict meta-test is red without the parser-conformance change).

intent.problem: attack A3-adjacent (register F-15): parser-conformance silently test.skip'ed its whole app-parser half when the strip-types subprocess failed — so the A3 claim 'parsers provably agree' could go green in CI without ever comparing the parsers.
intent.approach: in CI (CI=true, set by GitHub Actions) or under NOVAKAI_CONFORMANCE_STRICT, an unavailable app parser is a FAILING test naming the strict rule; locally the lenient, visible skip stays. NOVAKAI_FORCE_APP_UNAVAILABLE seam makes the unavailable path testable; conformance-strict.test.mjs spawns the real suite in both modes.
intent.rationale: strict-in-CI, lenient-locally is the register's prescribed shape; the forced-unavailable seam turns an environment-dependent failure mode into a deterministic fixture.
